_meta.description: Eco3min macro regime classification threshold table. Version this file with semantic versioning. Any modification = version bump + changelog entry. Reproducibility depends on threshold immutability for a given version.

changelog.note: Calibration audit by full-grid backtest 1968-2026 (sensitivity sweeps, episode scoring against a pre-registered ground-truth matrix, calibration/validation split by episode). ALL NUMERIC THRESHOLDS CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: G+ +0.10 (grid -0.05..+0.20: no episode discrimination; trades G_plus share 52->28% vs Transition share 15->33%); G- -0.50 (grid -0.30..-0.80 step 0.05: NBER capture 8/8 at EVERY value because entries are gate/threshold redundant; -0.50 dominates on latency, 2001: 0 months vs 3 at -0.70 and 1990-91: 3 vs 4, with maximum robustness margin to the 2022/2024 non-recession minima -0.34/-0.31; -0.35 rejected, margin 0.01-0.04 fragile to revisions); I+ 2.75 (2021 switch Oct-Nov, zero I_plus 2010-2019 at all tested values, 99% Grande Inflation coverage); I- 1.50 (unique value keeping 2013-2016 at 92% I_neutral while covering the Dec 2009-Apr 2011 deflation-risk episode 17/17 months; 1.75 flips 2013-16 to 96% I_minus, 1.25 loses 3 episode months); NFCI -0.50/+0.50/+1.50 (acute +1.50 uniquely yields GFC 7/7 acute months and ZERO stray acute post-1990; +1.25 adds 1 stray, +1.75 loses 1 GFC month); confirmation_months 2 (1 and 2 indistinguishable on history - MA3 smoothing; 3 degrades: state carry-over artifacts, +9 FP months, +latency). TWO BEHAVIOURAL CHANGES: (1) Sahm gate LEVEL rule -> UPWARD-CROSSING (edge) trigger. Entry unchanged (immediate G_minus on crossing 0.50; 8/8 captures, identical latencies avg 2.8 / max 4 months); persistence now governed by the normal state machine. Removes post-recession tails: false-positive G_minus months 55 -> 4, out-of-recession Stagflation months 19 -> 2; corrects published history rows 2009-09..2010-07 (Slowdown -> Transition), 2020-08..2021-04 (Slowdown -> Balanced Expansion), 2024-09..2024-10 (Stagflation -> Inflationary Pressure). Residual documented: Stagflation 2024-07/08 (real-time Sahm 0.53-0.57 did fire), Slowdown 1976-11/12 (Sahm touched exactly 0.50), and a 4-month G_neutral interlude inside the 1970 recession (degraded-mode ambiguity; an equivalent mid-recession dip already existed in 1973-75 under v1.0.0). (2) headline_underlying_divergence Brent trigger +20% -> dedicated +40% (HUD_BRENT_SHOCK), decoupled from the commodity_channel qualifier (unchanged at +/-20): activation frequency post-1988 falls 26.0% -> 17.9% of months, every genuine shock episode preserved incl. March-May 2026. Current verdict impact: NONE (Transition / accommodating / flag off before and after). See backtest_limitations.

Initial calibrated release. All PENDING_CALIBRATION values resolved from full historical distributions. Corrections vs thresholds_spec.json: (1) NFCI COVID/2022 episode references corrected — peak weekly NFCI in March 2020 was +0.27, never +2.0; 2022 NFCI never exceeded -0.10, never Restrictive. (2) CFNAI-MA3 G- percentile corrected to 10.6th, not 20-25th; value retained to block 2022 false positive. (3) PCE 2013-2015 reclassified as I_neutral in validation — Trimmed Mean was 1.49-1.72%, not I_minus. (4) Momentum override PCE explicitly excluded per architecture decision.

G_plus.percentile: 55th percentile of 1967-2026 CFNAI-MA3 distribution (707 obs). 45% of months classify as G_plus.
G_plus.basis: Buffers the Chicago Fed zero-trend boundary against noise. Validated: G_plus confirmed throughout 2021 (CFNAI-MA3 range +0.22 to +0.61) and H1 2022 (range +0.26). Does not trigger in late 2022-2024 slowdown (confirmed).

G_neutral.basis: Transition band. Captures 2022 (-0.22 to +0.26 range, never G_minus) and 2024-2026 slowdown (-0.26 to +0.03 range).

G_minus.percentile: 10.6th percentile of 1967-2026 CFNAI-MA3 distribution. Note: spec estimated 20-25th percentile — incorrect. The 20th percentile is -0.23, the 10th is -0.53. Value retained at -0.50 because: (a) does not trigger in 2022 non-recession (min 2022 = -0.34) (confirmed); (b) triggers in GFC (March 2008, 3 months after NBER December 2007 start — documented as ambiguous lag, not spec failure); (c) triggers immediately in COVID via Sahm gate override.
G_minus.basis: Sole value that simultaneously blocks the 2022 Stagflation false positive and confirms GFC 2008-2009. The 3-month lag vs NBER in 2007-2008 is documented as ambiguity, not a calibration error — the Sahm gate handles fast-onset recessions (COVID 2020).

episode_validation.GFC_2008: CFNAI-MA3 < -0.50 from March 2008 (3 months after NBER start Dec 2007). Lag documented as ambiguity. (confirmed) confirms eventually.
episode_validation.COVID_2020: Sahm gate triggers immediately (Feb/Mar 2020). CFNAI-MA3 itself reaches -1.52 in March 2020, -7.53 in April 2020. (confirmed)
episode_validation.2022_non_recession: CFNAI-MA3 minimum in 2022 = -0.34 (December 2022 on the July 2026 vintage; June 2022 reads -0.22 — CFNAI is revised, the monthly attribution moves across vintages). Never crosses -0.50. No G_minus, no Stagflation. (confirmed)

sahm_recession_override.effect: v1.1.0 EDGE TRIGGER: immediate G_minus on the UPWARD CROSSING of the threshold, regardless of CFNAI-MA3 — entry bypasses hysteresis entirely (unchanged vs v1.0.0). Persistence of G_minus is then governed by the normal state machine (CFNAI-MA3 candidate + 2-month confirmation); a fall of Sahm back below the threshold re-arms the gate.
sahm_recession_override.basis: Sahm (2019) institutional standard for recession ONSET — not a state indicator: the rule stays elevated deep into recoveries. The v1.0.0 level rule therefore held G_minus long after troughs (Stagflation 1991-07..1992-12, Slowdown 2020-08..2021-04 with CFNAI-MA3 +4.6, Stagflation 2024-07..10 with no NBER recession). Edge trigger validated on 1968-2026: 8/8 NBER captures, entry latencies unchanged (COVID April 2020 (confirmed), GFC April 2008 (confirmed)), false-positive G_minus months 55 -> 4.

sos_early_warning.effect: If CFNAI-MA3 is in G_neutral band AND SOS >= 0.20, upgrade candidate state to G_minus before hysteresis check.
sos_early_warning.basis: O'Trakoun & Scavette, Economics Letters 2025. 7 recessions correctly signaled since 1971. Current (May 2026): SOS = 0.000 — no signal.

icsa_corroboration.effect: Corroboration signal only — logged in regime_current.json as icsa_corroboration_triggered boolean. Does not override axis classification.
icsa_corroboration.percentile_note: 15% YoY increase in initial claims is a conservative signal threshold. No percentile calibration required for a corroboration-only input.

momentum_override.reason: Architecture decision (v1.0 design). Simple rule = reproducible = defensible. Reintroduce in v1.1 with explicit calibration window.

inflation_axis.treatment: 12-month rate as published by Dallas Fed. No transformation.

I_plus.percentile: 61.5th percentile — 38.5% of months classify as I_plus.
I_plus.basis: Fed 2% target + 75 bps buffer. Validated: 2022 (range 3.70-4.99%) confirms I_plus. 2021 December (3.36%) confirms I_plus. January 2025 (2.77%) borderline I_plus (confirmed). March 2026 (2.36%) I_neutral (confirmed).

I_neutral.basis: Fed comfort zone ± buffer. Captures 2013-2016 (range 1.49-1.72% — almost entirely I_neutral, not I_minus as incorrectly expected in earlier spec draft), 2023-2026 disinflation path (range 2.36-4.85%).
I_neutral.note_on_2013_2016: The 2013-2016 period is classified as I_neutral (low end of band), not I_minus. Trimmed Mean PCE sustained between 1.49% and 1.72% — below target but not deflation risk. This contradicts the earlier backtest_framework.md expectation. Corrected here.

I_minus.percentile: 4.1th percentile — rare. Only 24 months out of 579 in the I_minus zone. Captures genuine deflation-risk episodes only.
I_minus.basis: Sustained below target; deflation risk zone. Validated: September 2009 (1.48%) confirms I_minus. January 2015 (1.49%) borderline I_minus. The rarity (4th percentile) is intentional — I_minus should flag genuine disinflationary contraction, not ordinary below-target phases.

momentum_override.reason: Architecture decision (v1.0 design). Excluded from both I_plus and I_minus in v1.0.

t5yifr.availability_note: NOT available pre-2003. Secondary confirmation only — never overrides primary PCE classification.

stress_overlay.treatment: Monthly average of weekly Friday values. NOTE: this treatment yields significantly lower values than weekly peaks. The reference episodes below are based on monthly averages, not intra-month peaks.
stress_overlay.distribution_note: 665 monthly observations (1971–2026). p5=-0.77, p10=-0.71, p30=-0.56, p50=-0.39, p70=-0.03, p80=+0.41, p90=+1.40, p93=+2.02. Constructed with mean=0, std=1 over 1971+ sample, but distribution is right-skewed (crisis tail extends to +5.18).
stress_overlay.critical_note_on_episode_references: Previous spec references to 'COVID March 2020: NFCI peak ~+2.0' and '2022: NFCI peak ~+0.9' are INCORRECT. Actual data: COVID March 2020 monthly average NFCI = +0.09 (max weekly = +0.27 on March 27, 2020). 2022 NFCI remained negative throughout (range -0.55 to -0.10 monthly). The NFCI correctly reflects that financial conditions remained loose in 2022 despite rate hikes (excess COVID-era liquidity). These corrections affect the validation table but not the threshold values.
stress_overlay.hy_oas_note: HY OAS (BAMLH0A0HYM2) is NOT a primary input in v1.0. FRED truncates ICE BofA series to 3 years from April 2026. Check at runtime: if data/bamlh0a0hym2_history.csv exists → log as corroboration only; if absent → proceed with NFCI alone.

accommodating.percentile: 38.3% of months historically are accommodating — this reflects the structural loose-conditions bias of post-1990 monetary policy.
accommodating.basis: NFCI below historical average by 0.5 std dev. Validated: 2021 full year (range -0.54 to -0.69 monthly) (confirmed). Current May 2026: -0.51 confirms accommodating.

episode_validation.2021_QE_maximum: NFCI monthly average range -0.54 to -0.69. Confirmed accommodating.
episode_validation.2022_tightening: NFCI monthly range -0.55 to -0.10. Transitions from accommodating to neutral mid-2022 — NEVER reaches restrictive. This is the correct reading: financial conditions remained loose despite rate hikes.
episode_validation.COVID_2020: NFCI monthly average March 2020 = +0.09, April = +0.25. NOT accommodating in March-April 2020. The Sahm gate handles recession classification; overlay = neutral for COVID peak months.
episode_validation.GFC_2008: NFCI transitions: restrictive in September 2008 (+1.39), then acute_stress from October 2008 (+2.47) through early 2009. (confirmed)

neutral.basis: Default state. No prefix added to regime name.

restrictive.label_FR: dans un contexte de conditions financières restrictives
restrictive.percentile: NFCI > 0.50 occurs in 18.5% of months (p81). NFCI > 1.50 in 9.3% of months (p91).
restrictive.basis: NFCI above historical average. Historically associated with credit tightening cycles. Note: 2022 did NOT reach restrictive on monthly average basis despite 525bps of rate hikes.

acute_stress.percentile: 9.3% of months (p91). GFC peak: +2.47 to +2.98 monthly average (Oct-Dec 2008). (confirmed)
acute_stress.basis: Rare crisis-level stress. GFC 2008-2009 only confirmed episode in calibration window. COVID 2020 does NOT reach acute_stress on monthly NFCI basis.

hysteresis.rule: A candidate state change is confirmed only after 2 consecutive monthly periods with primary inputs beyond the threshold. The overlay updates immediately each month.

sahm_hard_gate.effect: Immediate G_minus on UPWARD CROSSING (v1.1.0 edge trigger). No confirmation period on entry. Does NOT hold the state while the level stays above the threshold: exit is governed by the state machine.

us_cli.treatment: 3-month delta (current month minus 3 months prior). Sign determines direction.

synchronization.note: Use amplitude-adjusted (AA) variants. Directional rule is binary — no numeric threshold needed. Noise risk: if too noisy, apply 2-month confirmation before switching sync label (implement as optional flag in code).

strengthening.label_FR: dollar en appréciation (conditions mondiales plus restrictives)

weakening.label_FR: dollar en dépréciation (détente des conditions mondiales)

dollar_channel.basis: Market convention. ±3% over 3 months represents a directionally significant move without capturing noise. Validated: 2022 H2 dollar appreciation (confirmed) (DXY +15% in that period); 2020 H2 dollar weakness (confirmed).

commodity_channel.source: World Bank CMO (eco3min existing pipeline) — monthly, LBMA-consistent pricing

demand_destruction.label_FR: effondrement de la demande en matières premières

episode_validation.2022_shock: Brent YoY peaked at +70%+ in H1 2022. (confirmed) shock
episode_validation.2020_demand_destruction: Brent YoY reached -65% in April 2020. (confirmed) demand_destruction
episode_validation.2026_current: Brent YoY: March 2026 = +41.8%, April 2026 = +72.2%. Confirmed as a shock. Iran/Hormuz geopolitical driver.

acute.label_FR: stress aigu sur les marchés mondiaux

vix.basis: Widely used market convention. CBOE thresholds broadly accepted.

em_spreads.reason: ICE Data truncation (BAMLEMHBHYCRPIOAS). Reintroduce in v1.1.

ciss_eurozone.basis: ECB CISS ranges from 0 (no stress) to 1 (maximum stress). 0.30 ≈ 70th-75th percentile historically. Validated: Eurozone debt crisis 2011-2012 (CISS sustained above 0.40) (confirmed); post-2015 normal (CISS typically 0.05-0.15) (confirmed).

headline_underlying_divergence.rule: true when inflation_state in ['I_neutral', 'I_minus'] AND brent_yoy_pct > 40.0
headline_underlying_divergence.constant: HUD_BRENT_SHOCK — dedicated constant, decoupled from BRENT_SHOCK (commodity_channel qualifier, unchanged at +/-20)
headline_underlying_divergence.effect: Boolean flag in regime_current.json. Surfaces the headline/underlying inflation gap in shortcode divergence block. Does NOT modify the regime name or code.
headline_underlying_divergence.calibration_v1_1_0: At +20% the flag was active 26.0% of post-1988 months (incl. an 18-month stretch in 2004-2005) — an ordinary oil-bull-market indicator, not a shock signal. At +40%: 17.9% of months, every genuine shock episode preserved (1990 Gulf, 1999-2000, 2021 H1, March-May 2026). +50% tested: 11.6% but loses March 2026 (+41.8% YoY), the very month the current divergence episode began.
headline_underlying_divergence.current_status_july_2026: off since the June 2026 print (Brent YoY back near +20% and below the +40 trigger); was on March-May 2026 (+41.8 / +72.2 / +66.2%)

wu_xia_shadow_rate.reason: Atlanta Fed suspended updates April 2022 (last value: 0.21%, Feb 2022).
wu_xia_shadow_rate.backtest_use: Historical fixture for ZLB periods only: Dec 2008 - Dec 2015, Mar 2020 - Mar 2022. Store as data/wu_xia_history.csv. Splice with FEDFUNDS outside ZLB.

backtest_limitations.vintages: The backtest uses currently published (revised) FRED data, not real-time vintages — except SAHMREALTIME, real-time by construction. CFNAI and Trimmed Mean PCE are revised: the 2021 I_plus switch reads October 2021 on the July 2026 vintage vs December 2021 on the May 2026 vintage; the revised Trimmed Mean PCE never exceeds 2.75% in 2008 (no Stagflation interlude in the GFC, contrary to the original expectation). The NFCI is re-estimated weekly over its FULL history: the May 2026 monthly average moved from -0.515 (May 2026 production run) to -0.4998 (July 2026 re-estimation), flipping the overlay label at the -0.50 boundary — documented boundary-noise property of a no-hysteresis overlay, not a calibration defect. Acceptable for a monthly editorial card; not a real-time signal.
backtest_limitations.hy_oas: BAMLH0A0HYM2 not backtestable before 2023-05 (FRED 3-year truncation since April 2026; pre-2023 archive lost). NFCI sole overlay primary; HY OAS corroboration only.
backtest_limitations.sos_gate: Richmond Fed SOS historical series not retrievable at calibration time (CSV endpoints unavailable). The SOS gate (threshold 0.20, FIXED institutional) is NOT covered by this backtest and operates live-only.
backtest_limitations.brent_source: Backtest Brent = FRED MCOILBRENTEU fallback; production uses World Bank CMO. Small level differences possible near the +40% divergence trigger.
backtest_limitations.pre_2003: Rows before 2003-01 are data_quality=degraded (no T5YIFR, SOS; no Trimmed Mean PCE pre-1978, no NFCI pre-1971). Pre-1978 recessions are scored on the growth axis only.

fields.data_as_of: ISO date (most recent input data date)
fields.thresholds_version: string (semver, must match this file version)
fields.lagged_inputs: array of string (series codes of lagged inputs)

regime_history_csv.notes: 24 columns in the published output schema. data_quality=degraded for pre-2003 rows (missing T5YIFR, Net Liquidity, SOS, OECD CLI). NaN for missing inputs — no imputation.
